verses.text: Zophar, stung by Job's warning of judgment, hastens to answer out of his agitation. His pride is wounded and must reply. A heart more eager to defend itself than to learn rushes quickly into speech.
Zophar, stung by Job's warning of judgment, hastens to answer out of his agitation. His pride is wounded and must reply. A heart more eager to defend itself than to learn rushes quickly into speech.
Zophar, stung by Job's warning of judgment, hastens to answer out of his agitation. His pride is wounded and must reply. A heart more eager to defend itself than to learn rushes quickly into speech.

The triumph of the wicked, he insists, is brief; though he climb to the clouds, he perishes like his own refuse, and none can find him. Zophar proclaims the short-lived glory of the godless. The truth is real, but he hurls it as an accusation at Job.

Such a man flies away like a dream, his bones full of the sins of his youth, laid down with him in the dust. His seeming greatness vanishes. The pleasures of sin leave only ruin to lie down with in the grave.

Though wickedness is sweet in his mouth and hidden under his tongue, it turns to the gall of asps within him. Sin's first taste deceives. What goes down as honey ferments into deadly poison in the soul.

He swallows down riches but must vomit them up, for God will cast them out of his belly; he shall not enjoy the rivers of honey. Ill-gotten gain cannot be kept. What is gotten by wrong God will wrench away again.

He swallows down riches but must vomit them up, for God will cast them out of his belly; he shall not enjoy the rivers of honey. Ill-gotten gain cannot be kept. What is gotten by wrong God will wrench away again.

He swallows down riches but must vomit them up, for God will cast them out of his belly; he shall not enjoy the rivers of honey. Ill-gotten gain cannot be kept. What is gotten by wrong God will wrench away again.

He must give back his gains unenjoyed, because he crushed and forsook the poor and seized houses he did not build. His oppression returns upon him. God marks every wrong done to the needy and repays it.

He must give back his gains unenjoyed, because he crushed and forsook the poor and seized houses he did not build. His oppression returns upon him. God marks every wrong done to the needy and repays it.

Feeling no quietness within, in the fullness of his plenty he is brought into straits, and every hand of trouble comes upon him. Greed knows no rest. The covetous heart is never satisfied and never at peace.

Feeling no quietness within, in the fullness of his plenty he is brought into straits, and every hand of trouble comes upon him. Greed knows no rest. The covetous heart is never satisfied and never at peace.

Feeling no quietness within, in the fullness of his plenty he is brought into straits, and every hand of trouble comes upon him. Greed knows no rest. The covetous heart is never satisfied and never at peace.

When about to fill his belly, God rains wrath upon him, and the arrow pierces him through. Judgment overtakes him at the height of his ease. The sinner's feast is interrupted by the stroke he never expected.

Darkness swallows his hidden treasures, the heavens reveal his iniquity, and the earth rises against him; 'this is the portion of a wicked man from God.' Zophar ends with the doom of the ungodly. True of the impenitent, but no verdict on the suffering saint before him.
